old_column.content: In Zod, validation errors are surfaced as instances of the `z.core.$ZodError` class.

new_column.content: In Zod, validation errors are surfaced as instances of the `z.core.$ZodError` class.

old_column.content: > The `ZodError` class in the `zod` package is a subclass that implements some additional convenience methods.

new_column.content: > The `ZodError` class in the `zod` package is a subclass that implements some additional convenience methods.

old_column.content: Instances of `$ZodError` contain an `.issues` array. Each issue contains a human-readable `message` and additional structured metadata about the issue.

new_column.content: Instances of `$ZodError` contain an `.issues` array. Each issue contains a human-readable `message` and additional structured metadata about the issue.

old_column.content: const result = z.string().safeParse(12); // { success: false, error: ZodError }

new_column.content: const result = z.string().safeParse(12); // { success: false, error: ZodError }

old_column.content: //     message: 'Invalid input: expected string, received number'

new_column.content: //     message: 'Invalid input: expected string, received number'

old_column.content: const result = z.string().safeParse(12); // { success: false, error: z.core.$ZodError }

new_column.content: const result = z.string().safeParse(12); // { success: false, error: z.core.$ZodError }

old_column.content: Every issue contains a `message` property with a human-readable error message. Error messages can be customized in a number of ways.

new_column.content: Every issue contains a `message` property with a human-readable error message. Error messages can be customized in a number of ways.

old_column.content: Virtually every Zod API accepts an optional error message.

new_column.content: Virtually every Zod API accepts an optional error message.

old_column.content: This custom error will show up as the `message` property of any validation issues that originate from this schema.

new_column.content: This custom error will show up as the `message` property of any validation issues that originate from this schema.

old_column.content: //       message: 'Not a string!'   <-- 👀 custom error message

new_column.content: //       message: 'Not a string!'   <-- 👀 custom error message

old_column.content: All `z` functions and schema methods accept custom errors.

new_column.content: All `z` functions and schema methods accept custom errors.

old_column.content: If you prefer, you can pass a params object with an `error` parameter instead.

new_column.content: If you prefer, you can pass a params object with an `error` parameter instead.

old_column.content: The `error` param optionally accepts a function. An error customization function is known as an **error map** in Zod terminology. The error map will run at parse time if a validation error occurs.

new_column.content: The `error` param optionally accepts a function. An error customization function is known as an **error map** in Zod terminology. The error map will run at parse time if a validation error occurs.

old_column.content: **Note** — In Zod v3, there were separate params for `message` (a string) and `errorMap` (a function). These have been unified in Zod 4 as `error`.

new_column.content: **Note** — In Zod v3, there were separate params for `message` (a string) and `errorMap` (a function). These have been unified in Zod 4 as `error`.

old_column.content: The error map receives a context object you can use to customize the error message based on the validation issue.

new_column.content: The error map receives a context object you can use to customize the error message based on the validation issue.

old_column.content: error: (iss) => iss.input === undefined ? "Field is required." : "Invalid input."

new_column.content: error: (iss) => iss.input === undefined ? "Field is required." : "Invalid input."

old_column.content: For advanced cases, the `iss` object provides additional information you can use to customize the error.

new_column.content: For advanced cases, the `iss` object provides additional information you can use to customize the error.

old_column.content: iss.inst; // the schema/check that originated this issue

new_column.content: iss.inst; // the schema/check that originated this issue

old_column.content: iss.path; // the path of the error

new_column.content: iss.path; // the path of the error

old_column.content: Depending on the API you are using, there may be additional properties available. Use TypeScript's autocomplete to explore the available properties.

new_column.content: Depending on the API you are using, there may be additional properties available. Use TypeScript's autocomplete to explore the available properties.

old_column.content: iss.inclusive; // whether the minimum is inclusive

new_column.content: iss.inclusive; // whether the minimum is inclusive

old_column.content: return `Password must have ${iss.minimum} characters or more`;

new_column.content: return `Password must have ${iss.minimum} characters or more`;

old_column.content: Return `undefined` to avoid customizing the error message and fall back to the default message. (More specifically, Zod will yield control to the next error map in the [precedence chain](#error-precedence).) This is useful for selectively customizing certain error messages but not others.

new_column.content: Return `undefined` to avoid customizing the error message and fall back to the default message. (More specifically, Zod will yield control to the next error map in the [precedence chain](#error-precedence).) This is useful for selectively customizing certain error messages but not others.

old_column.content: To customize errors on a *per-parse* basis, pass an error map into the parse method:

new_column.content: To customize errors on a *per-parse* basis, pass an error map into the parse method:

old_column.content: This has *lower precedence* than any schema-level custom messages.

new_column.content: This has *lower precedence* than any schema-level custom messages.

old_column.content: The `iss` object is a [discriminated union](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/2/narrowing.html#discriminated-unions) of all possible issue types. Use the `code` property to discriminate between them.

new_column.content: The `iss` object is a [discriminated union](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/2/narrowing.html#discriminated-unions) of all possible issue types. Use the `code` property to discriminate between them.

old_column.content: > For a breakdown of all Zod issue codes, see the [`zod/v4/core`](/packages/core#issue-types) documentation.

new_column.content: > For a breakdown of all Zod issue codes, see the [`zod/v4/core`](/packages/core#issue-types) documentation.

old_column.content: By default, Zod does not include input data in issues. This is to prevent unintentional logging of potentially sensitive input data. To include the input data in each issue, use the `reportInput` flag:

new_column.content: By default, Zod does not include input data in issues. This is to prevent unintentional logging of potentially sensitive input data. To include the input data in each issue, use the `reportInput` flag:

old_column.content: To specify a global error map, use `z.config()` to set Zod's `customError` configuration setting:

new_column.content: To specify a global error map, use `z.config()` to set Zod's `customError` configuration setting:

old_column.content: Global error messages have *lower precedence* than schema-level or per-parse error messages.

new_column.content: Global error messages have *lower precedence* than schema-level or per-parse error messages.

old_column.content: The `iss` object is a [discriminated union](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/2/narrowing.html#discriminated-unions) of all possible issue types. Use the `code` property to discriminate between them.

new_column.content: The `iss` object is a [discriminated union](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/2/narrowing.html#discriminated-unions) of all possible issue types. Use the `code` property to discriminate between them.

old_column.content: > For a breakdown of all Zod issue codes, see the [`zod/v4/core`](/packages/core#issue-types) documentation.

new_column.content: > For a breakdown of all Zod issue codes, see the [`zod/v4/core`](/packages/core#issue-types) documentation.

old_column.content: To support internationalization of error message, Zod provides several built-in **locales**. These are exported from the `zod/v4/core` package.

new_column.content: To support internationalization of error message, Zod provides several built-in **locales**. These are exported from the `zod/v4/core` package.

old_column.content: > **Note** — The regular `zod` library automatically loads the `en` locale automatically. Zod Mini does not load any locale by default; instead all error messages default to `Invalid input`.

new_column.content: > **Note** — The regular `zod` library automatically loads the `en` locale automatically. Zod Mini does not load any locale by default; instead all error messages default to `Invalid input`.

old_column.content: To lazily load a locale, consider dynamic imports:

new_column.content: To lazily load a locale, consider dynamic imports:

old_column.content: For convenience, all locales are exported as `z.locales` from `"zod"`. In some bundlers, this may not be tree-shakable.

new_column.content: For convenience, all locales are exported as `z.locales` from `"zod"`. In some bundlers, this may not be tree-shakable.

old_column.content: Below is a quick reference for determining error precedence: if multiple error customizations have been defined, which one takes priority? From *highest to lowest* priority:

new_column.content: Below is a quick reference for determining error precedence: if multiple error customizations have been defined, which one takes priority? From *highest to lowest* priority:

old_column.content: 1. **Schema-level error** — Any error message "hard coded" into a schema definition.

new_column.content: 1. **Schema-level error** — Any error message "hard coded" into a schema definition.

old_column.content: 2. **Per-parse error** — A custom error map passed into the `.parse()` method.

new_column.content: 2. **Per-parse error** — A custom error map passed into the `.parse()` method.

old_column.content: 3. **Global error map** — A custom error map passed into `z.config()`.

new_column.content: 3. **Global error map** — A custom error map passed into `z.config()`.

old_column.content: 4. **Locale error map** — A custom error map passed into `z.config()`.

new_column.content: 4. **Locale error map** — A custom error map passed into `z.config()`.
